title: After 10 Years of Failure and Wins, I Found Indicator that Works!

creator_notes.quote: The interpretation is just like any other types of moving averages.

creator_notes.quote: When the price is above the line, we can say that the market is trending up.

extracted_rules: When the price is above the line, we can say that the market is trending up.
And when the price is below the line, we can say that the market is trending down.
When the price is moving farther away from the moving average line, we can say that the upside momentum is strong.

And when it is moving close to the line, we say that the upside momentum is weakening which could be because of a pullback or in another situation after that a reversal may happen and the price may go below the line.

Similarly here when the price is moving farther away below the moving average line we can say that the downside momentum is strong and when it is moving near the line we say that the downside momentum is weakening which can be because of a pullback or a reversal may happen and the price may go above the line.
And when it is trending, it is either trending up or trending down.
When the market is in an uptrend, the price makes higher highs and higher lows.
And when the market is in a downtrend, the price makes lower highs and lower lows.
And if you don't see this structure, it means that the market is not trending.
When the market is trending, there is a concept that is related to breakouts inside the trends.
When we are in an uptrend, when the price goes for a pullback, before the pullback, a sewing high is created on the chart.
When this level is broken and we get a candle that has closed above this level, it can be a signal that probably the price is going to advance more in the direction of the uptrend.
Similarly, in a downtrend, when the price goes for a pullback, before the pullback, a swing low is created and this low can act as a potential support level.
So in practice, we could enter a long position at this point with the stop loss below the pullback or below the green line if it is not far from our trade entry and profit target at 1.5.

So the breakout here is confirmed and we could enter a long position at this point with the stop loss below the pullback or below the green line if it is not very far from our trade entry and profit target at 1.5 risk-to-reward ratio.
So in practice, we could enter a short position at this point with the stop loss above the moving average line and profit target at 1.5 risk-to-reward ratio.

So in practice we would enter a short position at this point with the stop loss above the pullback and profit target at 1.5 risk-to-reward ratio and until now the price is here and not known yet if this position will be profitable or not.
If this is just a pullback after that we will have decline of the price making this position profitable.
Losing possessions are always part of the game and you need to manage your risk properly so you can minimize the effect of your losing possessions.
There is a riskmanagement strategy that is called 1% rule and it is one of the best risk management strategies that you can use.
